citations.narrative:
The facility failed to handle a nonhuman primate as expeditiously and carefully as possible in a manner that does not
cause trauma, behavior stress, or unnecessary discomfort when staff did not recognize that the animal was in a transport
crate before discarding the crate. At approximately 10pm on January 28, 2026, the facility received a shipment of 300
nonhuman primates. Staff transferred the animals to quarantine enclosures. However, an animal care technician
incorrectly identified a crate as empty and removed it from the room, despite a nonhuman primate remaining inside. Staff
subsequently placed the crate in a disposal container which was transported offsite. An employee, of the third parter
disposal company, discovered the animal on February 2, 2026, when they opened the disposal bin to discard the crates.
The animal was darted, recovered, and identified as an animal belonging to the licensee. The animal was transported
back to the facility and later euthanized due to biocontainment reasons.
Improper verification of animal transfer and crate clearance can result in prolonged confinement and have a negative
impact on the health and well-being of the animal, including stress, injury, or death.
Handling of all animals shall be done as expeditiously and carefully as possible in a manner that does not cause trauma,
behavioral stress, physical harm, or unnecessary discomfort. Corrective measures were implemented by February 13,
2026.
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with facility representatives.
n

narrative:
2.131(b)(1)  Critical
   
Handling of animals. 
The facility failed to handle a nonhuman primate as expeditiously and carefully as possible in a manner that does not 
cause trauma, behavior stress, or unnecessary discomfort when staff did not recognize that the animal was in a transport 
crate before discarding the crate. At approximately 10pm on January 28, 2026, the facility received a shipment of 300 
nonhuman primates. Staff transferred the animals to quarantine enclosures. However, an animal care technician 
incorrectly identified a crate as empty and removed it from the room, despite a nonhuman primate remaining inside. Staff 
subsequently placed the crate in a disposal container which was transported offsite. An employee, of the third parter 
disposal company, discovered the animal on February 2, 2026, when they opened the disposal bin to discard the crates. 
The animal was darted, recovered, and identified as an animal belonging to the licensee. The animal was transported 
back to the facility and later euthanized due to biocontainment reasons. 
 
Improper verification of animal transfer and crate clearance can result in prolonged confinement and have a negative 
impact on the health and well-being of the animal, including stress, injury, or death. 
 
Handling of all animals shall be done as expeditiously and carefully as possible in a manner that does not cause trauma, 
behavioral stress, physical harm, or unnecessary discomfort. Corrective measures were implemented by February 13, 
2026. 
 
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with facility representatives.
